html: <div class='w-full h-full bg-white text-gray-900 px-12 py-8 font-sans flex flex-col'>
What a memory-only export carries
Typed inventory of a workspace nurtured over several weeks; export scope = conversation history + distilled context (memory-portability class). No cross-type weighting.
<table class='w-full text-[12px] leading-tight border-collapse'>

<thead><tr class='border-b-2 border-gray-800 text-left'><th class='py-1 pr-2 font-semibold'>Layer</th><th class='py-1 pr-2 font-semibold'>Artifact type</th><th class='py-1 pr-2 font-semibold text-right'>Count</th><th class='py-1 font-semibold'>Verdict</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
ConversationalMemory topic files216● carried
Chat transcripts276● carried (session metadata lost)
Conversation summaries199● carried
ApplicationCollection schemas (the applications)43○ lost
SKILL.md operating manuals43◔ degraded (text survives, function lost)
Collection records1,228◔ degraded (prose at best, inoperative as data)
Custom views (model-authored code)34○ lost
KnowledgeWiki pages153◔ degraded (link graph, history, rendering lost)
Wiki snapshot history247○ lost
BehavioralAutomations / scheduler entries88○ lost
Reminder / recurrence runtime state—○ lost
Feed definitions + ingest state12○ lost
MediaAttachments51○ lost
Generated artifacts (charts, docs)1,536○ lost
</tbody>

Functionally: 0 of 43 applications, 0 of 1,228 operative records, 0 of 88 automations survive. What survives in full is exactly the layer the assistant's conversations live in — and none of the layers its applications live in.
● carried (operative)  ◔ degraded (content survives, function lost)  ○ lost
